text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语学习栏目《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Georgie. As we get older, many of us notice our sense of hearing getting worse.
zh: 我是尼尔。我是乔治。随着年龄增长,我们很多人会发现自己的听力变差了。

text: Maybe we need to concentrate harder or ask people to speak more loudly. Has that happened to you, Neil?
zh: 也许我们需要更专心地听,或者请别人说话大声一点。这种情况在你身上发生过吗,尼尔?

text: Not really. A while ago I thought maybe my ears were getting worse, but I had a check -up and everything was fine, thankfully.
zh: 没怎么发生过。前一阵子我以为自己的听力可能变差了,但检查后一切正常,谢天谢地。

text: That's great. In the UK alone, hearing loss affects 18 million people, and that number is increasing.
zh: 那太好了。仅在英国,就有1800万人受听力损失影响,而且这个数字还在增加。

text: In this programme, we'll discover why we lose hearing with age, and as usual, learn some useful new vocabulary.
zh: 在这期节目中,我们将探讨为什么人会随着年龄增长而听力下降,并像往常一样学习一些实用的新词汇。

text: And you can also find a transcript of this episode on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你也可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期的文字稿。

text: But now listen up, Georgie, because I have a question for you.
zh: 不过现在,乔治,你听好了,因为我有个问题要问你。

text: Did you know that the The three smallest bones in the human body –
zh: 你知道吗,人体中最小的三块骨头——

text: the hammer, the anvil and the stirrup – are all located in the ear, but which one of those is the smallest?
zh: 锤骨、砧骨和镫骨——都位于耳朵里,那么其中哪一块最小呢?

text: Is it a the hammer, b the anvil, or c the stirrup bone?
zh: 是a锤骨,b砧骨,还是c镫骨?

text: That one's a difficult one. I'm going to say the anvil bone. OK, well we'll find out the answer later on.
zh: 这题有点难。我猜是砧骨。好,我们稍后揭晓答案。

text: To understand why our hearing gets worse with age, it's useful to know a little about how the ear works in the first place.
zh: 要理解为什么我们的听力会随年龄增长而变差,先了解一下耳朵最初是如何运作的会很有帮助。

text: Here's Mr Nish Mehta, an ear, nose and throat surgeon at the Royal National ENT Hospital in London,
zh: 这位是尼什·梅塔先生,伦敦皇家国立耳鼻喉医院的耳鼻喉科外科医生,

text: giving a beginner's class to BBC Radio 4 programme Inside Health.
zh: 他将为BBC第四台节目《健康内幕》做一次入门讲解。

text: Hearing is an amazing sense. It's a special sense. And the ear is really the hearing organ.
zh: 听觉是一种神奇的感觉,是一种特殊的感觉。耳朵正是听觉器官。

text: So the ear is split up into three main parts. It's the outer ear, the middle ear and the inner ear.
zh: 耳朵分为三大部分:外耳、中耳和内耳。

text: The outer ear is the bit that you see, so the side of your head. We call that the pinner.
zh: 外耳是你能看到的部分,也就是头部两侧的部分,我们称之为耳廓。

text: Think of that as the satellite dish. It collects the sounds and it funnels them in towards the sense organ.
zh: 可以把它想象成一个卫星天线,它收集声音,并将声音汇聚导向感觉器官。

text: That then gets transmitted to the middle ear. The middle ear's main job is to amplify those sounds.
zh: 然后声音被传送到中耳。中耳的主要作用是放大这些声音。

text: That's done by having an eardrum which catches it and then vibrates sympathetically to the vibration of the noise that you heard.
zh: 这是通过鼓膜实现的,鼓膜接收声音,并随着你听到的噪音的振动而共振。

text: And then that's passed on to the three smallest bones in the body, the hammer, the anvil and the stirrup.
zh: 接着声音被传递到人体中最小的三块骨头:锤骨、砧骨和镫骨。

text: The ear is made up of outer, middle and inner parts. It's one of our sense organs
zh: 耳朵由外耳、中耳和内耳组成,是我们感觉器官之一

text: – specific parts of the body – your ears, eyes, tongue, nose and skin – which allow you to experience the outside world.
zh: ——即身体的特定部位,比如耳朵、眼睛、舌头、鼻子和皮肤——它们让你能够感知外部世界。

text: The visible part – the outer ear or pinna – acts like a satellite dish, collecting sounds which it funnels into the middle ear.
zh: 可见的部分——外耳,也就是耳廓——就像一个卫星天线,收集声音并将其导入中耳。

text: The verb funnel means to move or direct something through a narrow space.
zh: 动词funnel意为通过狭窄的空间移动或引导某物。

text: Once in the middle ear, the eardrum amplifies the sounds,
zh: 一旦进入中耳,鼓膜会放大声音,

text: before transmitting them to tiny tiny hairs in the inner ear, which then sends electrical signals to the brain.
zh: 然后将声音传递给内耳中极其细小的毛发细胞,这些细胞再将电信号发送给大脑。

text: It's an incredible system, but unfortunately one that can go wrong at any stage.
zh: 这是一个不可思议的系统,但不幸的是,它在任何阶段都可能出问题。

text: Here, James Gallagher, presenter of BBC Radio 4's Inside Health, asks Nish Mehta when problems tend to start.
zh: 这里,BBC第四台《健康内幕》节目主持人詹姆斯·加拉格尔询问尼什·梅塔,听力问题通常从何时开始出现。

text: We're going to spend a lot of today, Nish, thinking about age -related hearing loss.
zh: 尼什,今天我们要花很多时间来讨论与年龄相关的听力损失。

text: When does that start? Wear and tear of your hearing starts from around the age of eight.
zh: 这从什么时候开始呢?听力的磨损大约从八岁左右就开始了。

text: OK, so we're doomed already. We're already on a downward spiral, but we've got a lot of redundancy on the system.
zh: 好吧,那我们已经注定完蛋了。我们已经在走下坡路了,不过我们的系统里有很多冗余。

text: And the speed at which our hearing declines is partly based on the genetics that you're born with,
zh: 我们听力衰退的速度部分取决于我们与生俱来的基因,

text: and then partly based on the environment that you're exposed to.
zh: 部分取决于我们所处的环境。

text: So someone who has really strong genetics to protect them from hearing, but spends their life on a pneumatic drill, may have a much faster decline than someone the other side.
zh: 所以有人基因非常强大,能很好地保护听力,但如果一辈子都在用风钻工作,他的听力可能会比另一些人衰退得快得多。

text: You can imagine that your brain is doing so much computation behind the scenes just to listen to someone speaking and understand the words that are saying.
zh: 你可以想象,大脑在幕后要做大量的运算,才能听懂别人说话,理解他们说的内容。

text: So whilst hearing is going, the brain tends to increase its listening effort.
zh: 所以当听力下降时,大脑往往会增加它的“听力努力”。

text: And so often it's a very slow process for you to realise that you're struggling with hearing.
zh: 因此,你往往要经过很长的过程,才会意识到自己在听力上有困难。

text: Wear and tear of our hearing starts as young as eight.
zh: 我们的听力磨损早在八岁就开始了。

text: Wear and tear means the damage that comes from ordinary everyday use.
zh: Wear and tear指的是日常使用所造成的损耗。

text: When Nish says this, James exclaims, we're doomed – an informal way of saying we're destined to fail.
zh: 当尼什这么说时,詹姆斯惊呼:我们完蛋了——这是一种非正式的说法,意思是我们注定要失败。

text: From the age of eight, our hearing is on a downward spiral – a situation which gets worse and worse.
zh: 从八岁起,我们的听力就开始走下坡路——情况变得越来越糟。

text: But don't worry just yet. Human hearing has evolved with redundancy in the system,
zh: 但先别担心。人类的听觉系统在进化中形成了冗余,

text: a technical phrase meaning that there are many ways to achieve the same outcome, so that a backup system can take over if other systems fail.
zh: 这是一个专业术语,意思是有多种方式可以达到同样的结果,这样当其他系统失效时,备用系统可以接管。

text: Here, for example, the brain works harder to compensate for a weaker sound signal reaching the inner ear.
zh: 比如说,当传到内耳的声音信号较弱时,大脑会更加努力地工作来弥补。

text: And there are other ways hearing loss can be helped as well, including hearing aids, surgery, or even just removing extra earwax.
zh: 此外,还有其他方法可以帮助改善听力损失,包括助听器、手术,甚至只是清理多余的耳垢。

text: Not a job I would like very much. After that fascinating journey into the world of hearing, I think it's time to reveal the answer to your question, Neil.
zh: 这可不是我很想做的工作。经过这趟精彩的听觉世界之旅之后,我想是时候揭晓你那个问题的答案了,尼尔。

text: I asked you which is the smallest bone in the body and they are all in the ear.
zh: 我问你人体中最小的骨头是哪一块,它们都在耳朵里。

text: Is it a the hammer, b the anvil or c the stirrup bone?
zh: 是a锤骨,b砧骨,还是c镫骨?

text: I said the anvil, didn't I? You did, but I'm afraid you obviously weren't listening in biology class because you are wrong.
zh: 我说的是砧骨,对吧?没错,不过恐怕你生物课根本没好好听讲,因为你答错了。

text: It is in fact the stirrup bone. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with sense organs
zh: 正确答案其实是镫骨。好了,让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,先从感觉器官开始

text: – specific parts of the body like your ears and eyes, which allow you to experience the world.
zh: ——即身体的特定部位,比如耳朵和眼睛,它们让你能够感知这个世界。

text: To funnel means to move or direct something through a narrow space.
zh: Funnel(动词)意思是通过狭窄的空间移动或引导某物。

text: And a funnel, the noun, is a cone -shaped tool which does this.
zh: 而funnel作为名词,是一种锥形工具,用来实现这种引导作用。

text: Wear and tear refers to the damage that comes from using something every day.
zh: Wear and tear指的是因日常使用而造成的损耗。

text: If you say, we're doomed, you mean we're going to be defeated or fail.
zh: 如果你说“we're doomed”,意思是我们将会失败或注定失败。

text: And a downward spiral is a situation which continuously gets worse and worse. And finally,
zh: 而downward spiral指的是持续恶化、越来越糟的情况。最后,

text: redundancy in the system refers to having more than one way to achieve an objective so that a backup can take over if other methods fail.
zh: redundancy in the system指的是有不止一种方式来实现某个目标,这样当其他方法失效时,可以有备用方案接管。

text: You'll be sad to hear that once again our six minutes are up,
zh: 你们可能会难过地发现,我们的六分钟又结束了,

text: but why not head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com to try the quiz and worksheet for this episode.
zh: 不过不妨去我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,试试本期的小测验和练习题。

text: Goodbye for now. Goodbye. from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 再见啦。再见。来自bbclearningenglish.com。
